system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling.Your task is to generate a complete and functional file named: <file_name>fvSolution</file_name> within the <folder_name>system</folder_name> directory. Ensure all required values are present and match with the files content already generated.Before finalizing the output, ensure:
- All necessary fields exist (e.g., if `nu` is defined in `constant/transportProperties`, it must be used correctly in `0/U`).
- Cross-check field names between different files to avoid mismatches.
- Ensure units and dimensions are correct** for all physical variables.
- Ensure case solver settings are consistent with the user's requirements. Available solvers are: ['compressibleInterFoam', 'cavitatingFoam', 'SRFSimpleFoam', 'solidEquilibriumDisplacementFoam', 'pimpleFoam', 'compressibleMultiphaseInterFoam', 'PDRFoam', 'reactingFoam', 'dsmcFoam', 'XiFoam', 'potentialFreeSurfaceFoam', 'foamyQuadMesh', 'chemFoam', 'electrostaticFoam', 'foamyHexMesh', 'simpleFoam', 'mdFoam', 'interFoam', 'potentialFoam', 'financialFoam', 'rhoPorousSimpleFoam', 'buoyantReactingFoam', 'rhoSimpleFoam', 'multiphaseEulerFoam', 'laplacianFoam', 'interMixingFoam', 'twoLiquidMixingFoam', 'boundaryFoam', 'pisoFoam', 'particleFoam', 'blockMesh', 'chtMultiRegionFoam', 'buoyantFoam', 'icoFoam', 'rhoPimpleFoam', 'mdEquilibrationFoam', 'rhoCentralFoam', 'porousSimpleFoam', 'solidDisplacementFoam', 'scalarTransportFoam', 'dnsFoam', 'refineMesh', 'SRFPimpleFoam', 'shallowWaterFoam', 'driftFluxFoam', 'adjointShapeOptimisationFoam', 'multiphaseInterFoam', 'mhdFoam', 'denseParticleFoam', 'moveDynamicMesh', 'snappyHexMesh'].
Provide only the code—no explanations, comments, or additional text.
user_prompt:
User requirement: do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 15m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-05.
Refer to the following similar case file content if helpful:
<similar_case_reference><index>
case name: pitzDaily
case domain: incompressible
case category: RAS
case solver: pimpleFoam
</index>
<directory_structure>
<dir>directory name: 0. File names in this directory: [U, epsilon, k, nuTilda, nut, p]</dir>
<dir>directory name: system. File names in this directory: [blockMeshDict, controlDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution]</dir>
<dir>directory name: constant. File names in this directory: [momentumTransport, physicalProperties]</dir>
</directory_structure></similar_case_reference>
match_level='high' use_scope="Use the matched pitzDaily/incompressible/RAS/pimpleFoam case as the primary template for all files including 0/ (boundary conditions), constant/ (transportProperties, turbulenceProperties, polyMesh), and system/ (controlDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution); adjust transportProperties (nu 1e-05), controlDict (deltaT 0.0001, writeInterval 0.01, endTime 0.3), and boundary condition values (inlet U 15 m/s, outlet pressure zeroGradient, wall noSlip) to match the user's specifications." advice='Directly adopt the matched pitzDaily RAS/pimpleFoam tutorial structure and modify only the turbulence model settings, mesh resolution for the millimeter-scale channel, transport viscosity, time-control parameters, and inlet velocity magnitude as specified by the user.'If the similar case is a weak match, do not copy it blindly. Use it only where it is consistent with the user requirement. Just modify the necessary parts to make the file complete and functional.Please ensure that the generated file is complete, functional, and logically sound.Additionally, apply your domain expertise to verify that all numerical values are consistent with the user's requirements, maintaining accuracy and coherence.When generating controlDict, do not include anything to preform post processing. Just include the necessary settings to run the simulation.
